pages.title: Introduction to Fruits
pages.text: Many different varieties of wild fruits can be found growing in the world. These can be collected to be eaten, or farmed, with the right equipment. These can be found on different varieties of bushes or trees.$(br2)Most fruiting plants have a common lifecycle. They will grow, form flowers, sprout fruit, and then lay dormant.

Fruit plants are seasonal. During their cold season, these plants will appear brown and lifeless. In the spring, they become green and healthy, getting ready to produce fruit and grow larger. The exact times this happen varies by the fruit. Fruit plants can die if kept outside the range of their $(l:tfc:the_world/climate#climate)Climate Conditions$().

$(thing)Fruit trees$() grow from tiny saplings into large, flowering trees. The branches of fruit trees are their heart, and they will grow as long as the climate conditions are right. As fruit trees mature, they will grow $(thing)leaves$() all around their branches. The leaves can flower and fruit depending on the $(l:tfc:the_world/climate#season)Season$().
pages.title: Fruit Trees

pages.text: A typical fruit tree.

Fruit trees start out as $(thing)Saplings$(). Saplings will only start growing―Placing their first piece of the tree trunk―If it is not their dormant season. The size of the finished tree is loosely determined by how many saplings are in the original sapling block.$(br)More saplings can be spliced onto a single block through $(thing)Grafting$(). To splice a sapling into another, just $(item)$(k:key.use)$() on it while holding a sapling and a $(thing)Knife$() in your off hand.

To get saplings from a fruit tree, break the 'elbow' branch blocks (90° angled branches) with an $(thing)Axe$(). Saplings can also be placed on these 'elbow' sections, if they are not too high up in the tree. This allows one fruit tree to grow multiple kinds of fruit. Harvesting fruit is done with $(item)$(k:key.use)$() when the leaf block is bearing fruit. This will give one fruit, and revert the plant back to its growing stage. Harvesting can be automated with $(item)Harvest Baskets$()
The fruiting calendar for fruit trees.
pages.title: Fruit Trees

pages.text: The fruiting calendar for fruit trees.

pages.text: An example plum tree.

$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#temperature)Temperature$(): 1 - 25 °C$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#calendar)Growth Days$(): 10$(br)$(bold)$(l:mechanics/hydration)Hydration$(): 12 - 100%$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#rainfall)Rainfall$(): 100 - 280mm$(br2)Red Apple trees grow from March to July, start flowering in August and September, and bear fruit in October.

pages.text: An example red apple tree.

$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#temperature)Temperature$(): 20 - 35 °C$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#calendar)Growth Days$(): 8$(br)$(bold)$(l:mechanics/hydration)Hydration$(): 26 - 100%$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#rainfall)Rainfall$(): 220 - 400mm$(br2)Cocoa trees are used to make $(l:firmalife/chocolate)Chocolate$().$(br2)They grow from December to March, start flowering in April and May, and bear fruit in June.
pages.title: Cocoa Tree

pages.text: An example cocoa tree.

$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#temperature)Temperature$(): 20 - 35 °C$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#calendar)Growth Days$(): 8$(br)$(bold)$(l:mechanics/hydration)Hydration$(): 15 - 100%$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#rainfall)Rainfall$(): 125 - 215mm$(br2)They grow from November to February, start flowering in March and April, and bear fruit in May.
pages.title: Fig Tree

pages.text: An example fig tree.

$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#temperature)Temperature$(): 17 - 35 °C$(br)$(bold)$(l:mechanics/hydration)Hydration$(): 33 - 100%$(br)$(bold)$(l:the_world/climate#rainfall)Rainfall$(): 280 - 500mm$(br2)Bananas are a special kind of fruit tree. They grow only vertically, lack leaves, and only fruit at the topmost block. Saplings are dropped from the flowering part of the plant. Once a banana plant is harvested, it dies, and will not produce any more fruit. It must be replanted in the spring.
pages.title: Banana Tree

pages.text: An example banana tree.

$(thing)Tall Bushes$() are fruit blocks that are able to grow in all directions, and spread. They do this by either growing directly upwards, up to three high, or placing $(thing)canes$() on their sides, which can mature into full bush blocks. After a while, the bushes will stop spreading, and reach maturity. Harvesting these bushes with a sharp tool has a chance to drop a new bush. Bushes that are fully mature will always drop themselves.
pages.title: Tall Bushes

pages.text: A wild tall bush.

Tall bushes are able to spread when their canes have somewhere to take root. Practically, this means that they need a solid block under them to place a new bush on. Providing a flat, open area free of grass or other debris gives them the best chance to grow.

Any mature bush block can grow berries, which are harvestable with $(item)$(k:key.use)$() or by using $(item)Harvest Baskets$().$(br2)Fruit bushes may also be placed in greenhouse $(item)Trellis Planters$() to grow year-round in any climate.
The fruiting calendar for berry bushes.

$(thing)Small Bushes$() are a kind of low lying fruit block that spawns in forests. Small bushes occasionally will spread to surrounding blocks, if there aren't too many other bushes nearby.$(br2)Small bushes will go through three sizes, and when grown, they are harvested just with $(item)$(k:key.use)$().
pages.title: Small Bushes

pages.text: Three different sizes of a healthy small bush
